name: eval-generation
whenToUse: Create new evaluation cases (hidden-pack or benchmark) with rubrics and verifiers for a capability or domain.
workflow: Define the capability and the failure modes the eval must catch.
Author cases conforming to the hidden-pack schema (id, domain, prompt, scoring).
For each case give mustInclude/mustAvoid + a semantic check and a verifier method.
Prefer auto-verifiable signals (exact answer, unit test, SQL result, citation) over prose.
Validate the pack with hidden_eval_protocol.py validate; dry-run the scorer.
Record the difficulty and the expected-failure baseline.
verification: Each case has at least one auto-checkable signal or a defined manual rubric.
Cases are not duplicates of existing visible benchmark items (no leakage).
commonFailures: Rubrics so loose any answer passes, or so strict none can.
Only prose checks with no auto-verifier.
Leaking cases from the public benchmark into the hidden pack.
Missing the expected-failure baseline, so deltas are meaningless.

examples.output: private/hidden-evals/toolops.json (5 cases, requiresToolLog), verifierTypes: [tool_log, mustInclude]; validate OK.
